steps.caption_md: nums = [1, 2, 3], total = 6, so target = 3 (half the total). Row 0 (no items considered) can only reach sum 0.
Item 1 (value 1) at s=1: is sum 1 reachable using just this first item?

predict.prompt_md: Considering only the first item (value 1), is sum 1 reachable — i.e. what is dp[1][1]?

choices.label_md: True — skip gives dp[0][1] = False, but take gives dp[0][0] = True, so dp[1][1] = True
False — dp[1][1] stays False since dp[0][1] is False
True, but only because dp[0][0] equals the target

predict.explanation_md: dp[i][s] is true if skipping works (dp[i-1][s]) OR taking works (dp[i-1][s - nums[i-1]]). Here s - 1 = 0, and dp[0][0] is always True, so dp[1][1] = True.

steps.caption_md: dp[1][1] = True — taking the single item of value 1 reaches sum 1.
By i=3 (all items considered), dp[3][3] = True: {1, 2} sums to 3 and the remaining {3} sums to 3 — the array can be partitioned into two equal-sum halves.
